goal: Return volume cm^3 and surface area cm^2 of a named fixture-insert prim, with watertight-mesh caveat.
thoughts: compute_volume needs a watertight mesh -- if non-closed, volume is garbage; flag this explicitly. Isaac Sim's native units are meters; convert outputs to cm^3 and cm^2 for SolidWorks parity. Do not hallucinate density or mass -- she asked for geometry.
code:
prims = list_all_prims(path_prefix="/World/Fixture/Insert_v2")
vol = compute_volume(prim_path="/World/Fixture/Insert_v2", units="m^3")
sa = compute_surface_area(prim_path="/World/Fixture/Insert_v2", units="m^2")
# Convert m -> cm
vol_cm3 = vol.value * 1_000_000
sa_cm2 = sa.value * 10_000
if not vol.is_watertight:
    note = "mesh not watertight -- volume unreliable"
else:
    note = "watertight mesh"
failure_modes: Returning volume for a non-watertight mesh without flagging the hole
Guessing density and handing her a mass number she did not request
Returning in m^3 without converting to cm^3 for SolidWorks parity
